reasoning: RELA encodes the p65 subunit of NF‑κB, a transcription factor that activates many genes involved in immune signaling and cell adhesion. When RELA is knocked down by CRISPRi, NF‑κB–dependent transcription is reduced. IGSF6 (immunoglobulin superfamily member 6) is expressed in immune cells and its promoter contains NF‑κB binding sites, indicating that its basal expression is at least partially driven by NF‑κB activity. Consequently, loss of RELA is expected to lower IGSF6 transcription.
